title: DALY rates from alcohol use disorders by age
subtitle: DALY (Disability-Adjusted Life Year) rates from alcohol use disorders by age, measured as the number of DALYs per 100,000 individuals. DALYs are used to measure total burden of disease - both from years of life lost and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.
caption:
DALY rates from alcohol use disorders by age

DALY (Disability-Adjusted Life Year) rates from alcohol use disorders by age, measured as the number of DALYs per 100,000 individuals. DALYs are used to measure total burden of disease - both from years of life lost and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.

This line chart tracks global Disability-Adjusted Life Year (DALY) rates from alcohol use disorders across six age groups from 1990 to 2023. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 600 DALYs per 100,000 individuals. The 50-69 years old group consistently shows the highest rates, starting near 460 in 1990, peaking above 530 around 1995, then declining to approximately 360 by 2023. The 15-49 years old group follows as the second-highest, beginning near 360, rising to about 420 in the mid-1990s, and falling to roughly 290 by 2023. The All ages and Age-standardized lines track closely together, both starting between 250 and 270, peaking near 300–310 around 1995, and ending near 220–230 in 2023. The 70+ years old group remains relatively flat throughout the period, hovering between 180 and 200. The 5-14 years old group stays at the bottom of the chart, with values consistently near zero across all years. Overall, every age group except the youngest shows a general downward trend after the mid-1990s, with the steepest declines occurring in the 50-69 and 15-49 age brackets. The gap between the highest and lowest adult age groups narrows over time, though the 50-69 cohort remains distinctly above all others through 2023.
